channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: The Eurovision Song Contest reaches its grand final with pop and protests
item.description: The final of the Eurovision Contest arrives Saturday, with tight security and rainy weather failing to dent the enthusiasm of fans, or the opposition of critics who think Israel shouldn't be invited.
item.content:encoded:
The final of the Eurovision Contest arrives Saturday, with tight security and rainy weather failing to dent the enthusiasm of fans, or the opposition of critics who think Israel shouldn't be invited.

(Image credit: Martin Meissner)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Gaza airstrike targeted Hamas military wing leader, Israel says
item.description: An Israeli airstrike in Gaza on Friday targeted the leader of Hamas' military wing, Israeli officials said, but it wasn't immediately clear if Izz al-Din al-Haddad was killed or injured.
item.content:encoded:
An Israeli airstrike in Gaza on Friday targeted the leader of Hamas' military wing, Israeli officials said, but it wasn't immediately clear if Izz al-Din al-Haddad was killed or injured.

(Image credit: Jehad Alshrafi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Iran has been confiscating property from critics of the regime
item.description: Iran's government has been confiscating property from people it deems traitors or critics of the regime.
item.content:encoded: Iran's government has been confiscating property from people it deems traitors or critics of the regime.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: CIA director's visit to Cuba echoes past memories of U.S. in Latin America
item.description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe makes a surprise visit to Cuba as the spy agency calls on the nation to make "fundamental changes." Cuba says it has run out of fuel and is suffering massive blackouts.
item.content:encoded: CIA Director John Ratcliffe makes a surprise visit to Cuba as the spy agency calls on the nation to make "fundamental changes." Cuba says it has run out of fuel and is suffering massive blackouts.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Shakira returns to official World Cup song duty, this time with Burna Boy
item.description: FIFA's official 2026 World Cup song is "Dai Dai" from Shakira and Burna Boy. There are a number of factors that shape which songs define a tournament — and endure beyond it.
item.content:encoded: FIFA's official 2026 World Cup song is "Dai Dai" from Shakira and Burna Boy. There are a number of factors that shape which songs define a tournament — and endure beyond it.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: SCOTUS upholds abortion pill telehealth access. And, Trump returns from China visit
item.description: The Supreme Court kept access to the abortion pill mifepristone available via telehealth. And, Trump says he made multiple trade deals during his state visit to China.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court kept access to the abortion pill mifepristone available via telehealth. And, Trump says he made multiple trade deals during his state visit to China.

(Image credit: Natalie Behring)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Key takeaways from Trump's China trip
item.description: President Trump returns to the U.S. after wrapping up his whirlwind trip to China.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump returns to the U.S. after wrapping up his whirlwind trip to China.

(Image credit: Evan Vucci)
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: China economist shares takeaways from the Trump-Xi summit
item.description: Steve Inskeep speaks with Chinese economist Keyu Jin about what came out of the Trump-Xi summit.
item.content:encoded: Steve Inskeep speaks with Chinese economist Keyu Jin about what came out of the Trump-Xi summit.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Death toll in attack on Kyiv apartment building now stands at 24
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy led an official day of mourning in Kyiv a day after a Russian cruise missile flattened an apartment building. It was one of the deadliest attacks on the capital in the 4-year-old war.
item.content:encoded:
Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy led an official day of mourning in Kyiv a day after a Russian cruise missile flattened an apartment building. It was one of the deadliest attacks on the capital in the 4-year-old war.

(Image credit: Efrem Lukatsky)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: How the relationship between the U.S. and China has changed under Trump
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep talks with Chinese scholar Da Wei about the history of the U.S.-China relationship and how it has changed under President Trump.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep talks with Chinese scholar Da Wei about the history of the U.S.-China relationship and how it has changed under President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep
